title: Bahuti Falls

overview.short: The Bahuti Falls is a waterfall in Mauganj district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: The Bahuti Falls is a waterfall in Mauganj district in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. It is the highest waterfall in Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Nai Garhi
topPlaces.description: Naigarhi is a rural town in the Mauganj district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It was founded by a Sengar Rajput king, Raja Chattradhari Singh.

items.note: Naigarhi is a rural town in the Mauganj district of Madhya Pradesh, India. It was founded by a Sengar Rajput king, Raja Chattradhari Singh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bahuti Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bahuti Falls is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bahuti Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Prayagraj Airport (Bamrauli) (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Prayagraj Junction (76 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bahuti Falls?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bahuti Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bahuti Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Bahuti Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bahuti Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nai Garhi.
